topic: Product Hunt Launch Best Practices for AI/SaaS Products
bullets: TIMING: Launch at 12:01 AM Pacific Time to maximise 24-hour exposure window. First 4 hours are critical — reach Top 4 before rankings stabilise.
TIMING: Tuesday-Thursday have highest traffic but fiercest competition. Monday or Friday recommended for optimising #1 ranking due to lower competition.
TIMING: For developer/AI tools, weekends can outperform mid-week due to higher browsing activity and better conversion rates.
TACTIC #1: Build 400+ seed supporters minimum before launch — quality over quantity. Established PH users carry 10x algorithmic weight.
TACTIC #2: Algorithm prioritises votes from verified, active users with contribution history. Engagement quality > raw upvote volume.
TACTIC #3: Ask for thoughtful feedback and comments, NOT upvotes. PH algorithm rewards engagement quality — comments keep you visible longer.
TACTIC #4: Reply to every comment on launch day with genuine, thoughtful responses. Top #1 winners (Ash, Magic Canvas, Raycast Focus) all did this.
TACTIC #5: Visual-first approach — gallery images must explain product workflow independently. Include short demo videos showing complete workflows.
TACTIC #6: Show results/outcomes, not feature lists. Before-and-after clips and real-world examples outperform feature dumps.
TACTIC #7: Reduce friction — enable first meaningful actions without requiring signup.
TACTIC #8: PH team now manually curates homepage (2025+). Products must demonstrate genuine value to get featured.
TACTIC #9: Spread supporters across multiple platforms (LinkedIn, X, Discord, Slack). Educate them on what valuable feedback looks like.
TACTIC #10: Pre-prep all content so launch day is 100% focused on engagement and responding to comments.
COMMUNITY: Product Hunt Global Slack — PH-specific launch coordination community.
COMMUNITY: Startup Chat Slack — one of the largest startup communities.
COMMUNITY: Indie Hackers — strong crossover with PH audience, high-quality early adopters.
COMMUNITY: r/SaaS and r/startups on Reddit — active founder communities.
COMMUNITY: Hacker News (Show HN) — technical audience, ideal for AI/dev tools.
COMMUNITY: SaaS Alliance Slack — SaaS founders and professionals.
COMMUNITY: Chatbots and AI Group Slack — AI/bot enthusiasts community.
COMMUNITY: Furlough Discord — collaborative entrepreneurship and digital marketing.
COMMUNITY: Dev.to — developer community, good for technical AI products.
COMMUNITY: X/Twitter AI and startup communities — build following via thought leadership pre-launch.
COMMUNITY: LinkedIn — connect with active PH users and build relationships weeks before launch.
TAGLINE: Formula — [Product] – [Verb] [Outcome] for [Audience] in [Time/Metric]. Example: 'Tally – Create typeforms in 30 seconds for indie hackers'.
TAGLINE: Must communicate value without requiring background knowledge. If 30%+ misunderstand, rewrite.
TAGLINE: Winning AI tagline patterns: 'AI designed for [specific use]' (Ash), 'Your first AI [team/assistant]' (Wegic), '[Known thing] meets [Known thing]' (21st.dev).
TAGLINE: Culture-focused framing can outperform feature-focused. Example: 'World's first vibe coding community' (YouWare).
TAGLINE: For AI tools, lead with the outcome, not the technology. 'Secure everything you build, host, and run' (Aikido) — no mention of AI in tagline.
BENCHMARK: Top-ranking products can expect ~1,500 unique visitors daily on launch.
BENCHMARK: Top 10 products often see 1,000+ signups on launch day.
WARNING: Coordinated voting schemes or purchased votes risk unfeaturing. PH actively penalises manipulation.
